summary: Applied ADR 0053 live from main by converging the monitoring VM tracing stack and the mail gateway runtime so Tempo search, service-graph metrics, and span metrics are all verified for the first traced internal API surface.

verification.observed: playbooks/monitoring-stack.yml limited to monitoring-lv3 completed from source commit 36516fa and rewrote the managed Prometheus unit with --enable-feature=exemplar-storage,native-histograms before confirming Prometheus, Tempo, and otelcol readiness.

monitoring-lv3 returned service.name values containing mail-gateway, traces_service_graph_request_total with client=user and server=mail-gateway, and traces_spanmetrics_calls_total series for gateway server, internal, and client spans.

verification.check: Mail gateway runtime rollout from main
verification.observed: playbooks/mail-platform.yml limited to docker-runtime-lv3 completed from main, rebuilt the lv3-mail-gateway image, rendered OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES=deployment.environment=lv3,service.namespace=lv3, and deployed telemetry.py with parse_resource_attributes into /opt/mail-platform/gateway/app.
monitoring-lv3 otelcol metrics reported accepted and sent spans after the rerun, and the queued tracing path no longer depends on the earlier manual Tempo port workaround.

notes: The first mail-platform converge attempt from main failed on intermittent guest transport before rendering changed runtime files; the immediate retry completed cleanly without requiring another manual guest-side patch.

The verified service-graph edge is currently the first traced API hop into mail-gateway, while outbound client spans to downstream systems are visible through span metrics and traces for follow-on instrumentation work.
